prompt:
A short length of 3 mm 2:1 polyolefin heat-shrink tubing is shown slipped over a freshly soldered wire joint on a small electronics project. The joint connects two color-coded insulated wires, and the tubing is partially shrunk so you can still see its matte surface hugging the connection while a section remains slightly expanded near the edge. A compact, pen-style heat source hovers nearby, out of focus, hinting that the tubing is about to be fully shrunk to insulate and protect the soldered joint.

Near-future solarpunk electronics workbench scene with clean, well-organized tools and a softly lit background of modular circuit boards and labeled bins. The aesthetic is high-tech but human-scale, with warm, cinematic lighting and subtle reflections on the tabletop. The image is photorealistic at 512×512 resolution, with no visible text or logos, and focuses clearly on the tubing and joint so the item reads instantly as heat-shrink used in careful DIY electronics.
